paper_title: Three-dimensional label-free imaging and analysis of Pinus pollen grains using optical diffraction tomography

methods.description: Process for collecting and preparing pollen grains from pine trees for imaging, ensuring they are in optimal condition for optical diffraction tomography (ODT).

steps.description: The pollen grains were collected during late April and May from blooming pine trees of a local farm.
steps.searchable_summary: collect pollen grains from pine trees

steps.description: Pollen grains were placed in containers by gently shaking them off the male cones of the trees.
The pollen grains were dried for approximately 2 hours at room temperature to prevent microbial invasion.
steps.searchable_summary: dry pollen grains at room temperature

steps.description: The containers with pollen grains were sealed and preserved in a dark cabinet at room temperature before imaging.
steps.searchable_summary: seal and store pollen grains in dark cabinet

steps.description: Pollen grains were suspended in a drop of RI-matching oil and sandwiched between two cover glasses.
steps.searchable_summary: suspend pollen grains in RI-matching oil

methods.method_name: Image Acquisition and 3D RI Map Reconstruction
methods.description: Procedure for acquiring holographic images and reconstructing 3D refractive index (RI) maps of pollen grains using optical diffraction tomography (ODT).

steps.description: Using ODT, the 3D RI map of a sample was reconstructed using holographic images of a sample that had been illuminated from different angles.
The acquisition of phase and intensity images was performed using an off-axis Mach-Zehnder interferometric setup with a digital micromirror device (DMD) installed.
steps.searchable_summary: acquire phase and intensity images using interferometric setup

steps.description: The incident angles of illumination were rapidly scanned by projecting a series of binary holograms on the installed DMD.
steps.searchable_summary: scan incident angles using binary holograms

steps.description: The light scattered by the sample was transmitted to the camera along an imaging system consisting of an objective lens and a tube lens.
The phase and amplitude of the scattered light can be retrieved from the recorded interference pattern, and then processed for reconstruction of the 3D RI map.
steps.searchable_summary: retrieve phase and amplitude from interference pattern

methods.description: Statistical analysis of pollen grain structures by extracting properties from 3D RI maps.

steps.description: The volume occupied by the pollen grain was determined using RI contrast.
steps.searchable_summary: determine pollen grain volume using RI contrast

steps.description: The surface area was calculated as the area of the outermost surface of the occupied region.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate surface area of pollen grain

steps.description: The sphericity index of a pollen grain was calculated from the volume and the surface area.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate sphericity index from volume and surface area

steps.description: The region occupied by starch granules was also segmented using RI contrast.
steps.searchable_summary: segment starch granules using RI contrast
